anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: This markdown binds axiom authoring to std_microcosm_axiom, the axiom doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because active law must be admitted through source routing and computed support, not asserted from projection prose.

skill_payload.mapping_basis: This markdown binds axiom authoring to std_microcosm_axiom, the axiom doctrine kind, and the executable doctrine grammar mechanism because active law must be admitted through source routing and computed support, not asserted from projection prose.

title: Microcosm Axiom Author
triggers: An axiom candidate needs admission into the governed axiom corpus rather than remaining residual pressure.
The agent can name routing authority, obligation evidence, support calculus coverage, anti-axiom separation, and migration boundaries.
workflow: Read std_microcosm_axiom, the relation registry, axiom routing registry, axiom support contract, anti-principle guards, and generated health before editing.
Admit only source-backed axiom rows or typed residual pressure; keep rejected anti-axioms separate from positive support.
Regenerate the axiom corpus and lattice projections, then run axiom, principle, standard, and broad doctrine checks before scoped commit.
